column6:
The Record ID represents the type of segment being reported.  The Record ID communicates how the contents of a 
given row of data should be interpreted depending on which segment type the Record ID represents.  Each type of 
segment collects different data elements so each segment type has a distinct layout. The first 3 characters identify 
the relevant file (e.g., ELG, PRV, CIP, etc.). The last 5 digits are the segment identifier padded with leading zeros (e.g., 
00001, 00002, 00003, etc.).
column21:
1. Mandatory
2. Value must be 8 characters
3. Value must be in Record ID List (VVL)
4. Value must equal "CIP00001"
column22:
"CIP00001"

  (1) FORM-TYP EQ "I"
      -OR-
      FORM-TYP EQ "O" AND CLM-COS EQ "20"
  (2) STA-EFF-DAT within the reporting month

column6: Indicates the type of adjustment record.
column21:
1. Value must be in Adjustment Indicator List (VVL)
2. If associated Type of Claim (CE) value is in [ 1, 3, 5, A, C, E, U, W, Y ], then value must be in [ 0, 1, 4 ]
3. If associated Type of Claim (CE) value is '4, D, X', then value must be in [ 5, 6 ]
4. Value must be 1 character
5. Mandatory
column22:
ENC+CLM:

  "0" = Original Claim/Encounter (default)
  "1" = Void
  "4" = Adjustment

  SELECT FIRST ACTION                                                
  WHEN DW-COMMON-S.ADJU-STA = 'V'                                  
       SET HDR-CLAIM-REC.CHDR-ADJ-INDC = '1' :VOID                 
  WHEN DW-COMMON-S.ADJU-STA = 'A'                                  
   AND DW-COMMON-S.PRR-CRN  GT $SPACES                             
       SET HDR-CLAIM-REC.CHDR-ADJ-INDC = '4' :ADJUSTMENT          :
  WHEN OTHER                                                       
       SET HDR-CLAIM-REC.CHDR-ADJ-INDC = '0' :ORIGINAL ENC(DEFAULT)
ENDSELECT                                                          

Note: If DW-COMMON-S.ADJU-STA EQ 'A' and the PRR-CRN field is populated, the CRN represents a replacement for a previously submitted encounter/claim and the PRR-CRN represents the replaced encounter.

column6:
The ICD-9/10-CM Diagnosis Code provided at the time of admission by the physician. Since the Admitting Diagnosis is formulated before all tests and examinations are complete, it may be stated in the form of a problem or symptom and it may differ from any of the final diagnoses 
recorded in the medical record.
column21:
1. When populated, a Diagnosis Code Flag is required
2. If associated Diagnosis Code Flag value is "1" (ICD-9), then value must be in  ICD-9 Diagnosis Code List (VVL)
3. If associated Diagnosis Code Flag value is "2" (ICD-10), then value must be in  ICD-10 Diagnosis Code List (VVL)
4. Value must be a minimum of 3 characters
5. Value must not contain a decimal point
6. If associated Diagnosis Code Flag value is '"1" (ICD-9), value must not exceed 5 characters
7. If associated Diagnosis Code Flag value is "2" (ICD-10), value must not exceed 7 characters
8. When there is more than one diagnosis code on a claim, each value must be unique
9. Conditional

column6: This code describes the area of specialty for the provider being reported.
column21:
1. Value must be in Provider Specialty List (VVL).
2. Value must be 2 characters
3. Conditional
column22:
ENC & CLM:

HDR-CLAIM-REC.CHDR-ADMT-PROV-NUM is set from DW-COMMON-S.ATND-PR-ID

 IF HDR-CLAIM-REC.CHDR-ADMT-PROV-NUM GT $SPACES          
   SET W.PR-ID        = HDR-CLAIM-REC.CHDR-ADMT-PROV-NUM 
   SET W.TMSIS-PR-TYP = HDR-CLAIM-REC.CHDR-ADMT-PROV-TYPE    
   SET W.PR-TAXONOMY  = HDR-CLAIM-REC.CHDR-ADMT-PROV-TAXONOMY
   DO P601-SPECIALTY                                        
   SET HDR-CLAIM-REC.CHDR-ADMT-PROV-SPEC = W.TMSIS-PR-SPEC   
 ELSE                                                       
   SET HDR-CLAIM-REC.CHDR-ADMT-PROV-SPEC = $SPACES           
 ENDIF                                                      

Note: 
(1) The ADMT Provider ID is not an AHCCCS required field.  
(2) The ADMT Provider Specialty is not an AHCCCS required field.
(3) Not all Providers have Specialties.
(4) The PR-SPEC mapping data can be found in HPMMIS online at RF976 & RF972

(5) Code within P601-SPECIALTY is complex and should be referenced within program IM52L007.

column6:
For services received during a single encounter with a provider, the date the service covered by this claim was received. For services involving multiple encounters on different days, or periods of care extending over two or more days, this would be the date on which the service 
covered by this claim began. For capitation premium payments, the date on which the period of coverage related to this payment began. For financial transactions reported to the OT file, populate with the first day of the time period covered by this financial transaction.
column21:
1. Value must be 8 characters in the form "CCYYMMDD"
2. The date must be a valid calendar date (i.e. Feb 29th only on the leap year,  never April 31st or Sept 31st)
3. When Type of Claim is not in ['2', '4', 'B', 'D', 'V'] value must be less than or equal to associated End of Time Period value
4. Value must be less than or equal to associated Ending Date of Service value
5. When Type of Claim is not in ['2', '4', 'B', 'D', 'V'] value must be less than or equal to associated Adjudication Date value
6. Value must be less than or equal to associated Date of Death (ELG.002.025) value when populated
7. Value must be less than or equal to at least one of the eligible's Enrollment End Date (ELG.021.254) values
8. Mandatory

column6: A code to describe the type of provider being reported.
column21:

column22:
ENC+CLM:

IF DW-COMMON-S.BIL-PR-TYP GT $SPACES
SET W.PR-TYP = DW-COMMON-S.BIL-PR-TYP
SET W.TMSIS-PR-TYP = $SPACES                            
                                                        
IF W.PR-TYP GT $SPACES                                  
  LOOP VARYING W.SUB FROM 1 BY 1                        
    UNTIL RF971-TBL.AHCCCS-CODE(W.SUB) LE $SPACES       
      IF RF971-TBL.AHCCCS-CODE(W.SUB) EQ W.PR-TYP       
        SET W.TMSIS-PR-TYP = RF971-TBL.TMSIS-CODE(W.SUB)
        QUIT PROCEDURE                                  
      ENDIF                                             
  ENDLOOP                                               
  SET HDR-CLAIM-REC.CHDR-BIL-PROV-TYPE = W.TMSIS-PR-TYP
ENDIF
                                                   
IF DW-COMMON-S.BIL-PR-TYP not GT $SPACES
  IF HDR-CLAIM-REC.CHDR-BILLING-PROV-NUM GT $SPACES      
    SET W.PR-ID = HDR-CLAIM-REC.CHDR-BILLING-PROV-NUM    
    DO P007-PR-DEM-ID                                    
    SET HDR-CLAIM-REC.CHDR-BIL-PROV-TYPE = W.TMSIS-PR-TYP
  ENDIF                                                   
ENDIF

if neither PR-ID nor PR-TYP are present 
SET HDR-CLAIM-REC.CHDR-BIL-PROV-TYPE = $SPACES

Notes:
(1) The Billing Provider Type is not an AHCCCS required field.
(2) The PR-TYP mapping data can be found in HPMMIS online at RF791 and RF975.

column6: The check or electronic funds transfer number.
column21:
1. Value must be 15 characters or less
2. Value must have an associated Check Effective Date
3. Value must not contain a pipe or asterisk symbols
4. Conditional
column22:
ENC: "8" FILL (not applicable)

CLM:
  IF DW-COMMON-S.PMT-AMT NE 0
    FOR FIRST FI-PAYMENT-R
      WHERE FI-PAYMENT-R.CRN    EQ DW-COMMON-S.CRN
        AND FI-PAYMENT-R.SEQ-NO EQ DW-COMMON-S.SEQ-NO
      
          SET W.INDEX   = $INDEX(FI-PAYMENT-R.INV-NO,SEA=‘ ’)
          SET W.INV-LEN = W.INDEX – 1
          SET W.PAD-LEN = 16 – W.INV-LEN
       
          SET W.INV-NO = $STRING 
           ($SUBSTR(FI-PAYMENT-R.INV-NO,STA=W.INDEX,LEN=W.PAD-LEN),
            $SUBSTR(FI-PAYMENT-R.INV-NO,STA=1,LEN=W.INV-LEN))
      
          FOR FIRST AP-INV-PMT-REC-R
            WHERE AP-INV-PMT-REC-R.INV-NBR EQ W.INV-NO
              IF AP-INV-PMT-REC-R.PMT-REF-NBR-1 EQ "00000000000"
                SET IM-TMSIS-CIP-S.CHDR-CHK-NUM = "8" FILL  :<-PROVIDER HAS CREDIT BALANCE
              ELSE
                SET IM-TMSIS-CIP-S.CHDR-CHK-NUM = AP-INV-PMT-REC-R.PMT-REF-NBR-1
                : remove all spaces or '-' from front of number
              ENDIF   
            WHEN NONE
              SET IM-TMSIS-CIP-S.CHDR-CHK-NUM = “999999999999999”
          ENDFOR
      
      WHEN NONE
        SET IM-TMSIS-CIP-S.CHDR-CHK-NUM = “999999999999999”  :THIS SHOULD NEVER OCCUR
    ENDFOR
  ELSE
    SET IM-TMSIS-CIP-S.CHDR-CHK-NUM = “888888888888888”  :NOT APPLICABLE FOR THIS CLAIM
  ENDIF

column6: The health care claim status codes convey the status of an entire claim status codes from the 277 transaction set. Only report the claim status for the final, adjudicated claim.
column21:

column22:

IF HDR-CLAIM-REC.CHDR-ADJ-INDC = '4' :ADJUSTMENT 
   SET HDR-CLAIM-REC.CHDR-CLAIM-STATUS-CAT = 'F3'
ELSE                                             
   SET HDR-CLAIM-REC.CHDR-CLAIM-STATUS-CAT = 'F0'
ENDIF

CLM:

  WHEN DW-COMMON-S.ADJU-STA = 'A'                       
       IF HDR-CLAIM-REC.CHDR-ADJ-INDC = '4' :ADJUSTMENT

          SET HDR-CLAIM-REC.CHDR-CLAIM-STATUS-CAT = 'F1'

  WHEN DW-COMMON-S.ADJU-STA = 'D'

ENDSELECT

column6: The general category of the claim status (accepted, rejected, pended, finalized, additional information requested, etc.), which is then further detailed in the companion data element claim status.
column21:
1. Value must be in Claim Status Category List (VVL)
2. (Denied Claim) if associated Claim Denied Indicator indicates the claim was denied, then value must be "F2"
3. (Denied Claim) if associated Claim Status is in [ 542, 585, 654 ], then value must be "F2"
4. Value must be 3 characters or less
5. Mandatory
column22:
ENC:
  IF ADJUSTMENT-IND (COT025) EQ "4"
    SET CLAIM-STATUS-CATEGORY = "F3"
  ELSE
    SET CLAIM-STATUS-CATEGORY = "F0"
  ENDIF

CLM:
  SELECT DW-COMMON-S.ADJU-STA
    WHEN "A"
      IF ADJUSTMENT-IND (COT025) EQ "4"
        SET CLAIM-STATUS-CATEGORY = "F3"
      ELSE 
        SET CLAIM-STATUS-CATEGORY = "F1"
      ENDIF
    WHEN "D"  SET CLAIM-STATUS-CATEGORY = "F2"
    WHEN "V"  SET CLAIM-STATUS-CATEGORY = "F4"
  ENDSEL

Values:
  FO = Finalized - The encounter has completed the adjudication cycle & no more action will be taken
  F1 = Finalized/Payment - The claim has been paid
  F2 = Finalized/Denial - The claim has been denied
  F3 = Finalized/Revised - Adjudication information has been changed
  F4 = Finalized/Adjudication Complete - No payment forthcoming, the claim has been adjudicated and
       no further payment is forthcoming

column5: Combined Beneficiary Cost Sharing Paid Amount
column6:
The combined amounts the beneficiary or his or her representative (e.g., their guardian) paid towards their copayment, coinsurance, and/or deductible for the covered services on the claim. Only report this data element when the claim does not differentiate among 
copayment, coinsurance, and/or deductible payments made by the beneficiary. Do not include beneficiary cost sharing payments made by a third party/ies on behalf of the beneficiary.
column21:
1. Value must be between -99999999999.99 and 99999999999.99
2. Value must be expressed as a number with 2-digit precision (e.g. 100.50 )
3. Conditional

column6: An indicator specifying whether the claim is a crossover claim where a portion is paid by Medicare.
column21:
1. Value must be in Crossover Indicator List (VVL)
2. Value must be 1 character
3. Value must be in [0, 1] or not populated
4. If Crossover Indicator value is "1", the associated Dual Eligible Code (ELG.005.085) value must be in "01", "02", "04", "08", "09", or "10" for the same time period (by date of service)
5. Value must be 1 character
6. If the Type of Claim value is in ["1", "3", "A", "C"], then value is mandatory and must be reported.
7. Conditional
column22:
If Encounter, "0" = Not a Medicare Crossover (default)

If Claim

 OR HDR-CLAIM-REC.CHDR-TOT-MED-DEDUCT-AMT GT 0 
    SET HDR-CLAIM-REC.CHDR-CROSSOVER-IND = '1' Medicare Crossover 
 ELSE                                          
    SET HDR-CLAIM-REC.CHDR-CROSSOVER-IND = '0' Not a Medicare Crossover (default) 
 ENDIF
ENDIF

column6:
A code to identify conditions that are present at the time the order for inpatient admission occurs; conditions that develop during an outpatient encounter, including emergency department, observation, or outpatient surgery.
POA indicator is used to identify certain preventable conditions that are: (a) high cost or high volume or both, (b) result in the assignment of a case to a Diagnosis Related Group (DRG)* that has a higher payment when present as a secondary diagnosis, and (c) could reasonably 
have been prevented through the application of evidence-based guidelines.
*States that do not use the grouper methodology may use CMS-approved methodology that is prospective in nature.
Each Diagnosis Code Flag is associated with one, and only one, Diagnosis Code in a given file segment record.  For example, Diagnosis Code n is associated with Diagnosis Code Flag n, where n can be any integer greater than or equal to 1.
column21:
1. Value must be in Diagnosis POA Flag List (VVL)
2. Value must be 1 character
3. Conditional
column22:
"8" FILL (default if DW-COMMON-S.DIAG-CD-1  is not present or if DW-COMMON-S.DIAG-POA-OTH-01 is '1')

This field is applicable only under specific conditions.  Since BLANK is a valid value, the default value is a SPACE.  The following is a list of valid values.  The field should be populated with whatever value is present; no editing required.

Valid Values:
  "Y" = Diagnosis was present at time of inpatient admission
  "N" = Diagnosis was not present at time of inpatient admission
  "U" = Documentation insufficient to determine if condition was present at time of inpatient admission
  "W" = Clinically undetermined
BLANK = Exempt from POA reporting (default)

Note: Since a value of "1" was a valid value at one time and has the same meaning as a BLANK entry, any POA field reported to AHCCCS with a value of "1" will be converted to a BLANK for TMSIS reporting.

POA indicator is used to identify certain preventable conditions that are: (a) high cost or high volume or both, (b) result in the assignment of a case to a Diagnosis Related Group (DRG)* that has a higher payment when present as a secondary diagnosis, and (c) could reasonably 
have been prevented through the application of evidence-based guidelines.
*States that do not use the grouper methodology may use CMS-approved methodology that is prospective in nature.
Each Diagnosis Code Flag is associated with one, and only one, Diagnosis Code in a given file segment record.  For example, Diagnosis Code n is associated with Diagnosis Code Flag n, where n can be any integer greater than or equal to 1.
column21:
1. Value must be in Diagnosis POA Flag List (VVL)
2. Value must be 1 character
3. Conditional
column22:
"8" FILL (default if DIAGNOSIS-CODE-10 is not present or if DW-COMMON-S.DIAG-POA-OTH-10 is '1')

This field is applicable only under specific conditions.  Since BLANK is a valid value, the default value is a SPACE.  The following is a list of valid values.  The field should be populated with whatever value is present; no editing required.

Valid Values:
  "Y" = Diagnosis was present at time of inpatient admission
  "N" = Diagnosis was not present at time of inpatient admission
  "U" = Documentation insufficient to determine if condition was present at time of inpatient admission
  "W" = Clinically undetermined
BLANK = Exempt from POA reporting (default)

Note: Since a value of "1" was a valid value at one time and has the same meaning as a BLANK entry, any POA field reported to AHCCCS with a value of "1" will be converted to a BLANK for TMSIS reporting.

column6:
The relative weight for the DRG on the claim. Each year CMS assigns a relative weight to each DRG. These weights indicate the relative costs for treating patients during the prior year. The national average charge for each DRG is compared to the overall average. This ratio is published annually in the Federal Register for each DRG. A DRG 
with a weight of 2.0000 means that charges were historically twice the average; a DRG with a weight of 0.5000 was half the average. This data element in T-MSIS is expected to capture the relative weight of the DRG in the state's system regardless of which DRG system the state uses.
column21:

column6:
This indicator indicates that the reimbursement amount included on the claim is for a fixed payment. Fixed payments are made by the state to insurers or providers for premiums or eligible coverage, not for a particular service. For example, some states have Primary Care Case Management 
programs where the state pays providers a monthly patient management fee of $3.50 for each eligible participant under their care. This fee is considered a fixed payment.  It is very important for states to correctly identify fixed payments. Fixed payments do not have a defined "medical record" 
associated with the payment; therefore, fixed payments are not subject to medical record request and medical record review.

column6:
The Medicare Beneficiary Identifier (MBI) is a randomly generated identifier used to identify all Medicare beneficiaries. It replaced the previously-used SSN-based Medicare HIC Number (HICN). To prevent identify theft, among other reasons, HICN gradually were retired and replaced by the MBI 
over the course of 2018 and 2019. Starting in 2020, the MBI became the primary identifier for Medicare beneficiaries.

column6:
The Medicare HIC Number (HICN) is an identifier formerly used by SSA and CMS to identify all Medicare beneficiaries. For many beneficiaries, their SSN was a major component of their HICN. To prevent identify theft, among other reasons, HICN gradually were retired and replaced by the 
Medicare Beneficiary Identifier (MBI) over the course of 2018 and 2019. HICN continue to be used by Medicare for limited administrative purposes after 2019 but starting in 2020 the MBI became the primary identifier for Medicare beneficiaries. HICN consists of two components: SSN & 
alpha-suffix or (for Railroad IDs) prefix and ID (not always SSN based)
column21:
1. Conditional
2. Value must be 12 characters or less
3. Value must not contain a pipe or asterisk symbols
4. (Not Dual Eligible) if Dual Eligible Code (ELG.DE.085) value = "00", then value must not be populated.
5. Value must be populated when Crossover Indicator (CIP.002.023) equals '1' and Medicare Beneficiary Identifier (CIP.002.222) is not populated.

    WHERE RP-MDC-CVG-R.AHCCCS-ID EQ DW-COMMON-S.PRI-AHCCCS-ID

        SET HDR-CLAIM-REC.CHDR-MED-HIC-NUM = RP-MDC-CVG-R.MDC-CLM-ID
      ENDIF
  ENDFOR

column22:

CLM:
  FOR EACH CL-RCVD-VAL-R

  ENDFOR

Notes:
(1) Zero is a valid amount for MEDICARE-PAID-AMT.
(2) Represents the aggregate of all detail lines.

column6: A state-assigned unique identification number used to identify a Medicaid/CHIP enrolled individual (except on service tracking payments). Value may be an SSN, temporary SSN or State-assigned eligible individual identifier. MSIS Identification Numbers are a unique 'key' value used to maintain referential integrity of data distributed over multiples files, segments and reporting periods. See T-MSIS Guidance Document, 'CMS Guidance: Reporting Shared MSIS Identification Numbers' for information on reporting the MSIS Identification Numbers ID for pregnant women, unborn children, mothers, and their deemed newborns younger than 1 year of age who share the same MSIS Identification Number. https://www.medicaid.gov/medicaid/data-and-systems/macbis/tmsis/tmsis-blog/entry/47572
column21:
1. Mandatory
2. For SSN States (i.e. SSN Indicator = 1), value must be equal to eligible individual's SSN
3. For Non-SSN States (i.e. SSN Indicator = 0), value must not be equal to eligible individual's SSN
4. Value must be 20 characters or less
5. When Type of Claim not in (4, D, X, Z, U, V, Y, W), value must match MSIS Identification Number (ELG.021.251) and the Admission Date (CIP.002.094) must be between Enrollment Effective Date (ELG.021.253) and Enrollment End Date (ELG.021.254)
6. When Type of Claim (CIP.002.100) equals 4, D or X (lump sum payment) value must begin with an '&'

column6:
The field denotes whether the payment amount was determined at the claim header or line/detail level.
For claims where payment is NOT determined at the individual line level (PAYMENT-LEVEL-IND = 1), the claim lines’ associated allowed (ALLOWED-AMT) and paid (MEDICAID-PAID-AMT) amounts are left blank and the total allowed (TOT-ALLOWED-AMT) and total paid (TOT-MEDICAID-PAID-AMT) amount is reported at the header level only.
For claims where payment/allowed amount is determined at the individual lines and when applicable, cost-sharing and/or coordination of benefits were deducted from one or more specific line-level payment/allowed amounts (PAYMENT-LEVEL-IND = 2), the allowed (ALLOWED-AMT) and paid (MEDICAID-PAID-AMT) amounts on the associated claim lines should sum to the total allowed (TOT-ALLOWED-AMT) and total paid (TOT-MEDICAID-PAID-AMT) amounts reported on the claim header.
For claims where payment/allowed amount is determined at the individual lines but then cost sharing or coordination of benefits was deducted from the total paid/allowed amount at the header only (PAYMENT-LEVEL-IND = 3), then the line-level paid amount (MEDICAID-PAID-AMT) would be blank and line-level allowed (ALLOWED-AMT) and header level total allowed (TOT-ALLOWED-AMT) and total paid (TOT-MEDICAID-PAID-AMT) amounts must all be populated but the line level allowed amounts are not expected to sum exactly to the header level total allowed.
For example, if a claim for an office visit and a procedure is assigned a separate line-level allowed amount for each line, but then at the header level a copay is deducted from the header-level total allowed and/or total paid amounts, then the sum of line-level allowed amounts may not be equal to the header-level total allowed amounts or correspond directly to the total paid amount. If the state cannot distinguish between the scenarios for value 1 and value 3, then value 1 can be used for all claims with only header-level total allowed/paid amounts.

column6:
A procedure code based on ICD-9 and ICD-10 used by the state to identify the procedures performed during the hospital stay referenced by this claim. The principal procedure and related info should be recorded in Procedure Code1, Procedure Code Date-1, and Procedure Code Flag 1.   The principal procedure is performed for definitive 
treatment rather than for diagnostic or exploratory purposes. It is closely related to either the principal diagnosis or to complications that arise during other treatments.  Use Procedure Code 2 through Procedure Code 6 (and related data elements) to record secondary, tertiary, etc. procedures.
column21:
1. When populated, there must be a corresponding Procedure Code Flag
2. If associated Procedure Code Flag List (VVL) value indicates an ICD-9-CM encoding '02', then value must be a valid ICD-9-CM procedure code
3. If associated Procedure Code Flag List (VVL) value indicates an ICD-10-CM encoding '07', then value must be a valid ICD-10-CM procedure code
4. If associated Procedure Code Flag List (VVL) value indicates an "Other" encoding '10-87', then State must provide T-MSIS system with State-specific procedure code list, and value must be a valid State-specific procedure code
5. Value must be 8 characters or less
6. Value must be in Procedure Code List (VVL)
7. Conditional
column22:
"8" FILL (default if not present)

Note: If present, a Procedure Code at the header level will be either an ICD-9 or ICD-10 Code and not a HCPCS Code.

column6: A code to indicate special Medicaid program under which the service was provided.

1. Value must be in Program Type List (VVL)
2. Value must be 2 characters

4. (Community First Choice) If value equals '11', then State Plan Option Type (ELG.011.163) must equal '01' for the same time period
5. If value equals '13', then State Plan Option Type (ELG.011.163) must equal '02' for the same time period
column22:
SELECT FIRST ACTION                                       
  WHEN DW-COMMON-S.FAM-PLN-IND = 'Y'                      
       SET HDR-CLAIM-REC.CHDR-PROGRAM-TYPE = '02'         
                                                          
  WHEN DW-COMMON-S.IHS-CD     = '1' OR '3'                
       SET HDR-CLAIM-REC.CHDR-PROGRAM-TYPE = '05'         
                                                          
  WHEN DW-COMMON-S.CRS-IND = 'Y'                          
       SET HDR-CLAIM-REC.CHDR-PROGRAM-TYPE = '12'         
                                                          
  WHEN OTHER                                              
       SET HDR-CLAIM-REC.CHDR-PROGRAM-TYPE = '00' :DEFAULT
ENDSELECT

column6:
A code to uniquely identify the geographic location where the provider's services were performed. The Provider Location Identifier values reported on Inpatient, Long-Term Care, Other, and Pharmacy Claim Header Segments must correspond to an active Provider Location Identifier 
value on a Provider Location and Contact Info (PRV.003) segment. If a particular license (e.g., a physician's medical license) or provider identifier (e.g., an individual provider's NPI or SSN) is applicable to all of their servicing locations, value "000" (a string of exactly three zeros) can 
be used in the PRV.004 or PRV.005, respectively, to represent all locations, however that location identifier must not be attributed to claims or provider bed type info.
column21:
1. Value must not contain a pipe or asterisk symbols
2. Value must be 5 characters or less
3. Mandatory
column26: TMSIS - 2019 Hawaii Source to Target Mapping Matrix.xlsx (sharepoint.com) , Data Guide from TMSIS Website Data Guide (medicaid.gov)

column6:
The Record ID represents the type of segment being reported.  The Record ID communicates how the contents of a 
given row of data should be interpreted depending on which segment type the Record ID represents.  Each type of 
segment collects different data elements so each segment type has a distinct layout. The first 3 characters identify 
the relevant file (e.g., ELG, PRV, CIP, etc.). The last 5 digits are the segment identifier padded with leading zeros (e.g., 
00001, 00002, 00003, etc.).
column21:
1. Mandatory
2. Value must be 8 characters
3. Value must be in Record ID List (VVL)
4. Value must equal "CIP00002"
column26: TMSIS - 2019 Hawaii Source to Target Mapping Matrix.xlsx (sharepoint.com) , Data Guide from TMSIS Website Data Guide (medicaid.gov)

column6: A sequential number assigned by the submitter to identify each record segment row in the submission file. The Record Number, in conjunction with the Record Identifier, uniquely identifies a single record within the submission file.
column21:
1. Value must be unique within record segment over all records associated with a given Record ID (CE)
2. Value must be 11 digits or less
3. Mandatory
column22: Begin with 1 and increment by 1 for every CIP00002/CIP00003 record written.

ID for this data element.

ENC & CLM:

HDR-CLAIM-REC.CHDR-REF-PROV-NUM is set from DW-COMMON-S.RFR-PR-ID

 IF HDR-CLAIM-REC.CHDR-REF-PROV-NUM GT $SPACES          
   SET W.PR-ID        = HDR-CLAIM-REC.CHDR-REF-PROV-NUM 
   SET W.TMSIS-PR-TYP = HDR-CLAIM-REC.CHDR-REF-PROV-TYPE    
   SET W.PR-TAXONOMY  = HDR-CLAIM-REC.CHDR-REF-PROV-TAXONOMY
   DO P601-SPECIALTY                                        
   SET HDR-CLAIM-REC.CHDR-REF-PROV-SPEC = W.TMSIS-PR-SPEC   
 ELSE                                                       
   SET HDR-CLAIM-REC.CHDR-REF-PROV-SPEC = $SPACES           
 ENDIF                                                      

Note: 
(1) The REFERRING Provider ID is not an AHCCCS required field.  
(2) The REFERRING Provider Specialty is not an AHCCCS required field.
(3) Not all Providers have Specialties.
(4) The PR-SPEC mapping data can be found in PMMIS online at RF976 & RF972

(5) Code within P601-SPECIALTY is complex and should be referenced within program IM52L007.

ENC+CLM:
"8" FILL (default)
                                                       
   SET W.PR-ID = HDR-CLAIM-REC.CHDR-REF-PROV-NUM           
   SET W.PR-TAXONOMY = $SPACES                            
   IF W.PR-ID GT $SPACES                                  
      FOR EACH PR-TAXONOMY-U NO UPDATE                     
        WHERE PR-TAXONOMY-U.STA   EQ 'A'                   
          AND PR-TAXONOMY-U.PR-ID EQ W.PR-ID               
            IF PR-TAXONOMY-U.TAX-SW EQ 'Y'                 
              SET W.PR-TAXONOMY = PR-TAXONOMY-U.TAXONOMY-CD
              QUIT PROC                                    
            ENDIF                                          
        WHEN NONE                                          
         :CONTINUE PROCESSING                              
      ENDFOR                                                                                                       
   ENDIF                                                  
   SET HDR-CLAIM-REC.CHDR-REF-PROV-TAXONOMY = W.PR-TAXONOMY    
 ENDIF

ENC+CLM:  

IF DW-COMMON-S.RFR-PR-TYP GT $SPACES
SET W.PR-TYP = DW-COMMON-S.RFR-PR-TYP
SET W.TMSIS-PR-TYP = $SPACES                            
                                                        
IF W.PR-TYP GT $SPACES                                  
  LOOP VARYING W.SUB FROM 1 BY 1                        
    UNTIL RF971-TBL.AHCCCS-CODE(W.SUB) LE $SPACES       
      IF RF971-TBL.AHCCCS-CODE(W.SUB) EQ W.PR-TYP       
        SET W.TMSIS-PR-TYP = RF971-TBL.TMSIS-CODE(W.SUB)
        QUIT PROCEDURE                                  
      ENDIF                                             
  ENDLOOP                                               
  SET HDR-CLAIM-REC.CHDR-REF-PROV-TYPE = W.TMSIS-PR-TYP
ENDIF
                                                   
IF DW-COMMON-S.REF-PR-TYP not GT $SPACES
  IF HDR-CLAIM-REC.CHDR-REF-PROV-NUM GT $SPACES      
    SET W.PR-ID = HDR-CLAIM-REC.CHDR-REF-PROV-NUM    
    DO P007-PR-DEM-ID                                    
    SET HDR-CLAIM-REC.CHDR-REF-PROV-TYPE = W.TMSIS-PR-TYP
  ENDIF                                                   
ENDIF

if neither PR-ID nor PR-TYP are present 
SET HDR-CLAIM-REC.CHDR-REF-PROV-TYPE = $SPACES

column6:
Remittance Advice Remark Codes are used to convey information about remittance processing or to provide a supplemental explanation for an adjustment already described by a Claim Adjustment Reason Code. Each Remittance Advice Remark Code identifies a specific message as 
shown in the Remittance Advice Remark Code List. It is a code set used by the health care industry to convey non-financial information critical to understanding the adjudication of a health care claim for payment. It is an external code set whose use is as mandated by the 
Administrative Simplification provisions of the Health Insurance Portability and Accountably Act of 1996 (P.L.104-191, commonly referred to as HIPAA).
column21:

column6:
The Remittance Advice Number is a sequential number that identifies the current Remittance Advice (RA) produced for a provider. The number is incremented by one each time a new RA is generated. The first five (5) positions are Julian date following a YYDDD format. The RA is the detailed 
explanation of the reason for the payment amount.
column21:
1. Value must be 30 characters or less
2. First five (5) characters of the value must be a Julian date express in the form YYDDD (e.g. 19095, 95th day of 20(19))
3. Value must not contain a pipe or asterisk symbols
4. Mandatory
column22:
ENC: $SPACE FILL (not applicable)

CLM:
   "8" FILL (default)
   IF DW-COMMON-S.PMT-AMT NE 0
    FOR FIRST FI-PAYMENT-R
      WHERE FI-PAYMENT-R.CRN EQ DW-COMMON-S.CRN
        AND FI-PAYMENT-R.SEQ-NO EQ DW-COMMON-S.SEQ-NO
          IF $SUBSTR(FI-PAYMENT-R.INV-NO,STA=2) GT $SPACES
              SET HDR-CLAIM-REC.CHDR-REMIT-NUM =  $SUBSTR(FI-PAYMENT-R.INV-NO,STA=2)       
          ELSE                                            
              SET HDR-CLAIM-REC.CHDR-REMIT-NUM =  '999999999999999999999999999999'         
          ENDIF                                           
      WHEN NONE
       SET REMITTANCE-NUM = 9 FILL
     ENDFOR
   ENDIF

column6:
The claim header level maximum amount determined by the payer as being 'allowable' under the provisions of the contract prior to the determination of actual payment. On FFS claims the Allowed Amount is determined by the state's MMIS. On managed care encounters the Allowed Amount is 
determined by the managed care organization.
column21:
1. Value must be between -99999999999.99 and 99999999999.99
2. Value must be expressed as a number with 2-digit precision (e.g. 100.50 )
3. When populated and Payment Level Indicator = '2' then value must equal the sum of all claim line Allowed Amount values
4. Conditional
column22:
ENC: 

  FOR FIRST EC-FACL-CLM-R
    WHERE EC-FACL-CLM-R.EC-NUM EQ DW-COMMON-S.CLM-NO
     SELECT
      WHEN EC-FACL-CLM-R.HP-ALLOW-AMT GT 0       
        SET HDR-CLAIM-REC.CHDR-TOT-ALLOWED-AMT = EC-FACL-CLM-R.HP-ALLOW-AMT             
      WHEN EC-FACL-CLM-R.HP-APPR-AMT GT 0        
        SET HDR-CLAIM-REC.CHDR-TOT-ALLOWED-AMT = EC-FACL-CLM-R.HP-APPR-AMT              
      WHEN EC-FACL-CLM-R.HP-PAID-AMT GT 0        
        SET HDR-CLAIM-REC.CHDR-TOT-ALLOWED-AMT = EC-FACL-CLM-R.HP-PAID-AMT              
     ENDSEL
    multiply by -1 if result is LT zero

CLM: DW-COMMON-S.ALLOW-AMT

Notes:
(1) Zero is a valid amount for TOT-ALLOWED-AMT.
(2) Represents the aggregate of all detail lines.

column6:
The Record ID represents the type of segment being reported.  The Record ID communicates how the contents of a 
given row of data should be interpreted depending on which segment type the Record ID represents.  Each type of 
segment collects different data elements so each segment type has a distinct layout. The first 3 characters identify 
the relevant file (e.g., ELG, PRV, CIP, etc.). The last 5 digits are the segment identifier padded with leading zeros (e.g., 
00001, 00002, 00003, etc.).

column6:
A state-assigned unique identification number used to identify a Medicaid/CHIP enrolled individual (except on service tracking payments).  Value may be an SSN, temporary SSN or State-assigned eligible individual identifier.

MSIS Identification Numbers are a unique "key" value used to maintain referential integrity of data distributed over multiples files, segments and reporting periods.

See T-MSIS Guidance Document, "CMS Guidance:  Reporting Shared MSIS Identification Numbers" for information on reporting the MSIS Identification Numbers ID for pregnant women, unborn children, mothers, and their deemed newborns younger than 1 year of age who share the 
same MSIS Identification Number.
https://www.medicaid.gov/medicaid/data-and-systems/macbis/tmsis/tmsis-blog/entry/47572

column6:
For services received during a single encounter with a provider, the date the service covered by this claim was received. For services involving multiple encounters on different days, or periods of care extending over two or more days, this would be the date on which the service 
covered by this claim began. For capitation premium payments, the date on which the period of coverage related to this payment began. For financial transactions reported to the OT file, populate with the first day of the time period covered by this financial transaction.
column21:
1. Value must be 8 characters in the form "CCYYMMDD"
2. The date must be a valid calendar date (i.e. Feb 29th only on the leap year,  never April 31st or Sept 31st)
3. When Type of Claim is not in ['2', '4', 'B', 'D', 'V'] value must be less than or equal to associated End of Time Period value
4. Value must be less than or equal to associated Ending Date of Service value
5. When Type of Claim is not in ['2', '4', 'B', 'D', 'V'] value must be less than or equal to associated Adjudication Date value
6. Value must be less than or equal to associated Date of Death (ELG.002.025) value when populated
7. Value must be less than or equal to at least one of the eligible's Enrollment End Date (ELG.021.254) values
8. Mandatory
column26: TMSIS - 2019 Hawaii Source to Target Mapping Matrix.xlsx (sharepoint.com) , Data Guide from TMSIS Website Data Guide (medicaid.gov)

column6:
For services received during a single encounter with a provider, the date the service covered by this claim was received. For services involving multiple encounters on different days, or periods of care extending over two or more days, the date on which the service covered by this claim ended. For capitation premium payments, the date on 
which the period of coverage related to this payment ends/ended.  For financial transactions reported to the OT file, populate with the last day of the time period covered by this financial transaction.
column21:
1. Value must be 8 characters in the form "CCYYMMDD"
2. The date must be a valid calendar date (i.e. Feb 29th only on the leap year,  never April 31st or Sept 31st)
3. When Type of Claim is not in ['2', '4', 'B', 'D', 'V'] value must be less than or equal to associated End of Time Period value
4. Value must be greater than or equal to associated Beginning Date of Service value
5. When Type of Claim is not in ['2', '4', 'B', 'D', 'V'] value must be less than or equal to associated Adjudication Date value
6. Value must be less than or equal to associated Date of Death (ELG.002.025) value when populated
7. Value must be equal to or greater than associated Date of Birth (ELG.002.024) value
8. Mandatory

column6:
A code which identifies a specific accommodation, ancillary service or billing calculation (as defined by UB-04 Billing 
Manual). Revenue Code should be passed through to T-MSIS exactly as it was billed by the provider on the provider's 
837I or UB-04 claim. It is only required on Inpatient, Long-Term Care and Other Fee for Service claims and managed 
care encounters that have a valid Type of Bill value. It's not required on financial transactions or non-institutional claims.
column21:
1. Value must be in Revenue Code List (VVL)

column6: A code to categorize the services provided to a Medicaid or CHIP enrollee.
column22:
When the program is first initialized, read the following data into a working storage table:

  SET N = 0

  FOR EACH RFGT2RC
    WHERE RFGT2RC.STA-CD  EQ "A"
      AND RFGT2RC.TBL-TYP EQ "RF970"
        ADD 1 TO N
        SET W.COS(N) = RFGT2RC.TBL-CD   <- 2-byte AHCCCS Category of Service
        SET W.TOS(N) = RFGT2RC.TBL-DESC <- 3-byte TMSIS Type of Service
  ENDFOR 

  SET W.COS-TOS-TBL-END = N

Find the TMSISIP.TYPE-OF-SERVICE using the DW-COMMON-S.CLM-COS:

  SET TMSISIP.TYP-OF-SERVICE = $spaces (default)
 but if value is not found it will be set to "088"

  <<COS-TOS-TBL>>
  LOOP VARYING N FROM 1 BY 1 THRU W.COS-TOS-TBL-END
    IF DW-COMMON-S.CLM-COS EQ W.COS(N)
      SET TMSISIP.TYPE-OF-SERVICE = W.TOS(N)
      QUIT COS-TOS-TBL
    ENDIF
  ENDLOOP   

Note: The COS-TOS mapping data can be found in HPMMIS online at RF970.

column22:
ENC+CLM:  

IF DW-COMMON-S.SER-PR-TYP GT $SPACES
SET W.PR-TYP = DW-COMMON-S.SER-PR-TYP
SET W.TMSIS-PR-TYP = $SPACES                            
                                                        
IF W.PR-TYP GT $SPACES                                  
  LOOP VARYING W.SUB FROM 1 BY 1                        
    UNTIL RF971-TBL.AHCCCS-CODE(W.SUB) LE $SPACES       
      IF RF971-TBL.AHCCCS-CODE(W.SUB) EQ W.PR-TYP       
        SET W.TMSIS-PR-TYP = RF971-TBL.TMSIS-CODE(W.SUB)
        QUIT PROCEDURE                                  
      ENDIF                                             
  ENDLOOP                                               
  SET DTL-CLAIM-REC.CDTL-SERV-PROV-TYPE = W.TMSIS-PR-TYP
ENDIF
                                                   
IF DW-COMMON-S.SER-PR-TYP not GT $SPACES
  IF DTL-CLAIM-REC.CDTL-SER-PROV-NUM GT $SPACES      
    SET W.PR-ID = DTL-CLAIM-REC.CDTL-SER-PROV-NUM    
    DO P007-PR-DEM-ID                                    
    SET DTL-CLAIM-REC.CDTL-SER-PROV-TYPE = W.TMSIS-PR-TYP
  ENDIF                                                   
ENDIF

if neither PR-ID nor PR-TYP are present 
SET DTL-CLAIM-REC.CDTL-SER-PROV-TYPE = $SPACES

Notes:
(1) The PR-TYP-TBL is the common routine for provider types. 
(2) The W.A-PR-TYP represents the AHCCCS-defined provider type.
(3) The W.T-PR-TYP represents the TMSIS-defined provider type.
(4) The PR-TYP mapping data can be found in HPMMIS online at RF791 and RF795.

column22:
If the BILLING-PROV-TAXONOMY (CIP181) data element is $SPACE filled
  $SPACE FILL
Else
  Use BILLING-PROV-TAXONOMY to determine the following:

    If “177F00000X” or “174200000X”, set to "170000000"  Non-Individual - Other Service Providers
    If first 2 positions equal “25”, set to "250000000"  Non-Individual - Agencies
    If first 2 positions equal “26”, set to "260000000"  Non-Individual - Ambulatory Health Care Facilities
    If first 2 positions equal “27”, set to "270000000"  Non-Individual - Hospital Units
    If first 2 positions equal “28”, set to "280000000"  Non-Individual - Hospitals
    If first 2 positions equal “29”, set to "290000000"  Non-Individual - Laboratories
    If first 2 positions equal “30”, set to "300000000"  Non-Individual - Managed Care Organizations
    If first 2 positions equal “31”, set to "310000000"  Non-Individual - Nursing & Custodial Care Facilities
    If first 2 positions equal “32”, set to "320000000"  Non-Individual - Residential Treatment Facilities
    If first 2 positions equal “33”, set to "330000000"  Non-Individual - Suppliers
    If first 2 positions equal “34”, set to "340000000"  Non-Individual - Transportation Services
    If first 2 positions equal “38”, set to "380000000"  Non-Individual - Respite Care Facility
    If first 2 positions equal $SPACE, set to "999999999"
   All Others, set to "100000000"  Individuals or Groups of Individuals

Notes:
(1) Logic derived based on the crosswalk values defined in Appendix N of "T-MSIS DD APPENDICES V1.1 - Appendices" document.
(2) The CIP181 (BILLING-PROV-TAXONOMY) is not an AHCCCS required field and may or may not be present to determine the value of this field.

column6:
The Record ID represents the type of segment being reported.  The Record ID communicates how the contents of a 
given row of data should be interpreted depending on which segment type the Record ID represents.  Each type of 
segment collects different data elements so each segment type has a distinct layout. The first 3 characters identify 
the relevant file (e.g., ELG, PRV, CIP, etc.). The last 5 digits are the segment identifier padded with leading zeros (e.g., 
00001, 00002, 00003, etc.).
column21:
1. Mandatory
2. Value must be 8 characters
3. Value must be in Record ID List (VVL)
4. Value must equal "CLT00002"

column6: A state-assigned unique identification number used to identify a Medicaid/CHIP enrolled individual (except on service tracking payments). Value may be an SSN, temporary SSN or State-assigned eligible individual identifier. MSIS Identification Numbers are a unique 'key' value used to maintain referential integrity of data distributed over multiples files, segments and reporting periods. See T-MSIS Guidance Document, 'CMS Guidance: Reporting Shared MSIS Identification Numbers' for information on reporting the MSIS Identification Numbers ID for pregnant women, unborn children, mothers, and their deemed newborns younger than 1 year of age who share the same MSIS Identification Number. https://www.medicaid.gov/medicaid/data-and-systems/macbis/tmsis/tmsis-blog/entry/47572
column21:
1. Mandatory
2. For SSN States (i.e. SSN Indicator = 1), value must be equal to eligible individual's SSN
3. For Non-SSN States (i.e. SSN Indicator = 0), value must not be equal to eligible individual's SSN
4. Value must be 20 characters or less
5. Populated value must begin with an '&', when TYPE-OF-CLAIM = 4, D or X (lump sum payment)
6. The Beginning Date of Service on the claim must fall between (ELG.021.253) enrollment effective and (ELG.021.253) end date
column26: TMSIS - 2019 Hawaii Source to Target Mapping Matrix.xlsx (sharepoint.com) , Data Guide from TMSIS Website Data Guide (medicaid.gov)

column6: An indicator specifying whether the claim is a crossover claim where a portion is paid by Medicare.
column21:
1. Value must be in Crossover Indicator List (VVL)
2. Value must be 1 character
3. Value must be in [0, 1] or not populated
4. If Crossover Indicator value is "1", the associated Dual Eligible Code (ELG.005.085) value must be in "01", "02", "04", "08", "09", or "10" for the same time period (by date of service)
5. Value must be 1 character
6. If the Type of Claim value is in ["1", "3", "A", "C"], then value is mandatory and must be reported.
7. Conditional

column6: Indicates the type of adjustment record.
column21:

column22:
ENC+CLM:

  "0" = Original Claim/Encounter (default)
  "1" = Void
  "2" = Re-submittal

  IF DW-COMMON-S.ADJU-STA EQ 'V'
    SET ADJUSTMENT-IND = "1"
  ELSE
    IF DW-COMMON-S.ADJU-STA EQ 'A' AND PRR-CRN GT " "
      SET ADJUSTMENT-IND = "2"
    ENDIF
  ENDIF 

Note: If DW-COMMON-S.ADJU-STA EQ 'A' and the PRR-CRN field is populated, the CRN represents a replacement for a previously submitted encounter/claim and the PRR-CRN represents the replaced encounter.

column6:
A code to identify conditions that are present at the time the order for inpatient admission occurs; conditions that develop during an outpatient encounter, including emergency department, observation, or outpatient surgery.
POA indicator is used to identify certain preventable conditions that are: (a) high cost or high volume or both, (b) result in the assignment of a case to a Diagnosis Related Group (DRG)* that has a higher payment when present as a secondary diagnosis, and (c) could reasonably 
have been prevented through the application of evidence-based guidelines.
*States that do not use the grouper methodology may use CMS-approved methodology that is prospective in nature.
Each Diagnosis Code Flag is associated with one, and only one, Diagnosis Code in a given file segment record.  For example, Diagnosis Code n is associated with Diagnosis Code Flag n, where n can be any integer greater than or equal to 1.
column21:
1. Value must be in Diagnosis POA Flag List (VVL)
2. Value must be 1 character
3. Conditional
column22:
"8" FILL (default if DIAGNOSIS-CODE-1 is not present)

This field is only applicable only under specific conditions.  Since BLANK is a valid value, the default value is a SPACE.  The following is a list of valid values.  The field should be populated with whatever value is present; no editing required.

Valid Values:
  "Y" = Diagnosis was present at time of inpatient admission
  "N" = Diagnosis was not present at time of inpatient admission
  "U" = Documentation insufficient to determine if condition was present at time of inpatient admission
  "W" = Clinically undetermined
BLANK = Exempt from POA reporting (default)

Note: Since a value of "1" was a valid value at one time and has the same meaning as a BLANK entry, any POA field reported to AHCCCS with a value of "1" will be converted to a BLANK for TMSIS reporting.

POA indicator is used to identify certain preventable conditions that are: (a) high cost or high volume or both, (b) result in the assignment of a case to a Diagnosis Related Group (DRG)* that has a higher payment when present as a secondary diagnosis, and (c) could reasonably 
have been prevented through the application of evidence-based guidelines.
*States that do not use the grouper methodology may use CMS-approved methodology that is prospective in nature.
Each Diagnosis Code Flag is associated with one, and only one, Diagnosis Code in a given file segment record.  For example, Diagnosis Code n is associated with Diagnosis Code Flag n, where n can be any integer greater than or equal to 1.

passed through to T-MSIS exactly as they were submitted by the provider on their claim (with the exception of removing

column6: The date on which the recipient was admitted to a psychiatric or long-term care facility.
column21:

4. Value must be greater than or equal to associated eligible Date of Birth (CE) value.
5. Value must be less than or equal to associated eligible Date of Death (CE) value.
6. Mandatory
7. When associated Type of Claim (CLT.002.052) is not '2','B' or 'V' (capitated payment) value must be before Adjudication Date (CLT.002.050)
8. When associated Type of Claim (CLT.002.052) is not '2','B' or 'V' (capitated payment) and Type of Service (CLT.003.211) is not '119, '120', '121', 122' value must be before Adjudication Date (CLT.003.233)

For capitation premium payments, the date on which the period of coverage related to this payment began. For financial transactions reported to the OT file, populate with the first day of the time period covered by this financial transaction.
column21:

column22:

    SET MEDICAID-PAID-DATE = DW-COMMON-S.HP-PAID-DAT
  ELSE
    SET MEDICAID-PAID-DATE = "8" FILL
  ENDIF

      SET MEDICAID-PAID-DATE = DW-COMMON-S.PMT-DAT
    ELSE
      IF DW-COMMON-S.PMT-AMT EQ 0
        SET MEDICAID-PAID-DATE = "8" FILL
      ENDIF
    ENDIF
  ENDIF

Notes:
1) The HP-PAID-DAT is not an AHCCCS required field for Encounters.
2) There is no PMT-DAT when there is no PMT-AMT for Claims.

column6: The general category of the claim status (accepted, rejected, pended, finalized, additional information requested, etc.) from the 277 transaction set which is then further detailed in the companion data element claim status.
column21:
1. Value must be in Claim Status Category List (VVL)
2. (Denied Claim) if associated Claim Denied Indicator indicates the claim was denied, then value must be "F2"
3. (Denied Claim) if associated Claim Status is in [ 542, 585, 654 ], then value must be "F2"
4. Value must be 3 characters or less
5. Mandatory
column22:
ENC:
  IF ADJUSTMENT-IND (COT025) EQ "2"
    SET CLAIM-STATUS-CATEGORY = "F3"
  ELSE
    SET CLAIM-STATUS-CATEGORY = "F0"
  ENDIF

CLM:
  SELECT DW-COMMON-S.ADJU-STA
    WHEN "A"
      IF ADJUSTMENT-IND (COT025) EQ "2"
        SET CLAIM-STATUS-CATEGORY = "F3"
      ELSE 
        SET CLAIM-STATUS-CATEGORY = "F1"
      ENDIF
    WHEN "D"  SET CLAIM-STATUS-CATEGORY = "F2"
    WHEN "V"  SET CLAIM-STATUS-CATEGORY = "F4"
  ENDSEL

Values:
  FO = Finalized - The encounter has completed the adjudication cycle & no more action will be taken
  F1 = Finalized/Payment - The claim has been paid
  F2 = Finalized/Denial - The claim has been denied
  F3 = Finalized/Revised - Adjudication information has been changed
  F4 = Finalized/Adjudication Complete - No payment forthcoming, the claim has been adjudicated and
       no further payment is forthcoming

column6: The check or electronic funds transfer number.
column21:
1. Value must be 15 characters or less
2. Value must have an associated Check Effective Date
3. Value must not contain a pipe or asterisk symbols
4. Conditional
column22:
ENC: "8" FILL (not applicable)

CLM:
  IF DW-COMMON-S.PMT-AMT NE 0
    FOR FIRST FI-PAYMENT-R
      WHERE FI-PAYMENT-R.CRN    EQ DW-COMMON-S.CRN
        AND FI-PAYMENT-R.SEQ-NO EQ DW-COMMON-S.SEQ-NO
      
          SET W.INDEX   = $INDEX(FI-PAYMENT-R.INV-NO,SEA=‘ ’)
          SET W.INV-LEN = W.INDEX – 1
          SET W.PAD-LEN = 16 – W.INV-LEN
       
          SET W.INV-NO = $STRING 
           ($SUBSTR(FI-PAYMENT-R.INV-NO,STA=W.INDEX,LEN=W.PAD-LEN),
            $SUBSTR(FI-PAYMENT-R.INV-NO,STA=1,LEN=W.INV-LEN))
      
          FOR FIRST AP-INV-PMT-REC-R
            WHERE AP-INV-PMT-REC-R.INV-NBR EQ W.INV-NO
              IF AP-INV-PMT-REC-R.PMT-REF-NBR-1 EQ '00000000000'
                SET IM-TMSIS-CLT-S.CHDR-CHK-NUM = "8" FILL  :<-PROVIDER HAS CREDIT BALANCE
              ELSE
                SET IM-TMSIS-CLT-S.CHDR-CHK-NUM = AP-INV-PMT-REC-R.PMT-REF-NBR-1
              ENDIF   
            WHEN NONE
              SET IM-TMSIS-CLT-S.CHDR-CHK-NUM = “999999999999999”
          ENDFOR
      
      WHEN NONE
        SET IM-TMSIS-CLT-S.CHDR-CHK-NUM = “999999999999999”
    ENDFOR
  ELSE
    SET IM-TMSIS-CLT-S.CHDR-CHK-NUM = “888888888888888”  :NOT APPLICABLE FOR THIS CLAIM
  ENDIF
column26: TMSIS - 2019 Hawaii Source to Target Mapping Matrix.xlsx (sharepoint.com) , Data Guide from TMSIS Website Data Guide (medicaid.gov)
column41:
ENC: "8" FILL (not applicable)

CLM:
  IF DW-COMMON-S.PMT-AMT NE 0
    FOR FIRST FI-PAYMENT-R
      WHERE FI-PAYMENT-R.CRN    EQ DW-COMMON-S.CRN
        AND FI-PAYMENT-R.SEQ-NO EQ DW-COMMON-S.SEQ-NO
      
          SET W.INDEX   = $INDEX(FI-PAYMENT-R.INV-NO,SEA=‘ ’)
          SET W.INV-LEN = W.INDEX – 1
          SET W.PAD-LEN = 16 – W.INV-LEN
       
          SET W.INV-NO = $STRING 
           ($SUBSTR(FI-PAYMENT-R.INV-NO,STA=W.INDEX,LEN=W.PAD-LEN),
            $SUBSTR(FI-PAYMENT-R.INV-NO,STA=1,LEN=W.INV-LEN))
      
          FOR FIRST AP-INV-PMT-REC-R
            WHERE AP-INV-PMT-REC-R.INV-NBR EQ W.INV-NO
              IF AP-INV-PMT-REC-R.PMT-REF-NBR-1 EQ '00000000000'
                SET IM-TMSIS-CLT-S.CHDR-CHK-NUM = "8" FILL  :<-PROVIDER HAS CREDIT BALANCE
              ELSE
                SET IM-TMSIS-CLT-S.CHDR-CHK-NUM = AP-INV-PMT-REC-R.PMT-REF-NBR-1
              ENDIF   
            WHEN NONE
              SET IM-TMSIS-CLT-S.CHDR-CHK-NUM = “999999999999999”
          ENDFOR
      
      WHEN NONE

column6:
Remittance Advice Remark Codes are used to convey information about remittance processing or to provide a supplemental explanation for an adjustment already described by a Claim Adjustment Reason Code. Each Remittance Advice Remark Code identifies a specific message as 
shown in the Remittance Advice Remark Code List. It is a code set used by the health care industry to convey non-financial information critical to understanding the adjudication of a health care claim for payment. It is an external code set whose use is as mandated by the 
Administrative Simplification provisions of the Health Insurance Portability and Accountably Act of 1996 (P.L.104-191, commonly referred to as HIPAA).
column21:

transactions.

column6:
This indicator indicates that the reimbursement amount included on the claim is for a fixed payment. Fixed payments are made by the state to insurers or providers for premiums or eligible coverage, not for a particular service. For example, some states have Primary Care Case Management 
programs where the state pays providers a monthly patient management fee of $3.50 for each eligible participant under their care. This fee is considered a fixed payment.  It is very important for states to correctly identify fixed payments. Fixed payments do not have a defined "medical record" 
associated with the payment; therefore, fixed payments are not subject to medical record request and medical record review.
column21:
1. Value must be 1 character
2. Value must be in [0, 1] or not populated

column6:
The field denotes whether the payment amount was determined at the claim header or line/detail level.
For claims where payment is NOT determined at the individual line level (PAYMENT-LEVEL-IND = 1), the claim lines’ associated allowed (ALLOWED-AMT) and paid (MEDICAID-PAID-AMT) amounts are left blank and the total allowed (TOT-ALLOWED-AMT) and total paid (TOT-MEDICAID-PAID-AMT) amount is reported at the header level only.
For claims where payment/allowed amount is determined at the individual lines and when applicable, cost-sharing and/or coordination of benefits were deducted from one or more specific line-level payment/allowed amounts (PAYMENT-LEVEL-IND = 2), the allowed (ALLOWED-AMT) and paid (MEDICAID-PAID-AMT) amounts on the associated claim lines should sum to the total allowed (TOT-ALLOWED-AMT) and total paid (TOT-MEDICAID-PAID-AMT) amounts reported on the claim header.
For claims where payment/allowed amount is determined at the individual lines but then cost sharing or coordination of benefits was deducted from the total paid/allowed amount at the header only (PAYMENT-LEVEL-IND = 3), then the line-level paid amount (MEDICAID-PAID-AMT) would be blank and line-level allowed (ALLOWED-AMT) and header level total allowed (TOT-ALLOWED-AMT) and total paid (TOT-MEDICAID-PAID-AMT) amounts must all be populated but the line level allowed amounts are not expected to sum exactly to the header level total allowed.
For example, if a claim for an office visit and a procedure is assigned a separate line-level allowed amount for each line, but then at the header level a copay is deducted from the header-level total allowed and/or total paid amounts, then the sum of line-level allowed amounts may not be equal to the header-level total allowed amounts or correspond directly to the total paid amount. If the state cannot distinguish between the scenarios for value 1 and value 3, then value 1 can be used for all claims with only header-level total allowed/paid amounts.

column6:
Indicates whether the claim is submitted by a provider or provider group enrolled in the Health Home care model to provide services for the beneficiary on the claim. Health home providers provide service for patients with chronic illnesses. States that do not specify an eligible individual's health home provider number, if applicable, should not 
report claims that indicate the claim is submitted by a provider or provider group enrolled in the health home model.
column21:
1. Value must be in Health Home Provider Indicator List (VVL)
2. Value must be 1 character

4. If there is an associated Health Home Entity Name value, then value must be "1"
5. Value must be 1 character
6. Conditional

4. Value must have a corresponding value in Waiver ID (CLT.002.129)
5. Conditional

column6:
Field specifying the waiver or demonstration which authorized payment for a claim. These IDs must be the approved, full federal waiver ID number assigned during the state submission and CMS approval process. Waiver IDs should actually only be the
"core" part of the waiver IDs, without including suffixes for renewals or amendments.
column21:
1. Value must be associated with a populated Waiver Type (CE)
2. Value must be 20 characters or less
3. (1115 demonstration waivers) If value begins with "11-W-" or "21-W-", the associated Claim Waiver Type value must be 01 or in [21-30]
4. (1915(b) or 1915(c) waivers) If value begins with the two-letter state abbreviation followed by a period (.), the associated Claim Waiver Type value must be in [02-20, 32, 33]
5. Conditional
column26: TMSIS - 2019 Hawaii Source to Target Mapping Matrix.xlsx (sharepoint.com) , Data Guide from TMSIS Website Data Guide (medicaid.gov)

column6:
A unique identification number assigned by the state to a provider or capitation plan. This data element should represent the entity billing for the service. For encounter records, if associated Type of Claim value equals 3, C, or W, then value must be the state identifier of the provider or entity 
(billing or reporting) to the managed care plan.
column21:
1. Value must be 30 characters or less
2. Conditional
3. When Type of Claim not in ('Z','3','C','W',"2","B","V"," 4","D","X") then value may match (PRV.002.019) Submitting State Provider ID or
4. When Type of Claim not in ('Z','3','C','W',"2","B","V"," 4","D","X") then value may match (PRV.005.081) Provider Identifier where the Provider Identifier
5. Ending Date of Service (CLT.002.049) may be between Provider Attributes Effective Date (PRV.002.020) and Provider Attributes End Date (PRV.002.021) or
6. Ending Date of Service (CLT.002.049) may be between Provider Identifier Effective Date (PRV.005.079) and Provider Identifier End Date (PRV.005.080)
column22:
CAP: "8" FILL (not applicable)
  
ENC+CLM:
  
  "8" FILL (default)
  
  IF  DW-COMMON-S.BIL-PR-NPI GT " "
  AND DW-COMMON-S.BIL-PR-NPI NE "9999999995"
  
    <<BIL-PR-ID>>
    FOR EACH PR-ALT-ID-R
      WHERE PR-ALT-ID-R.PR-STA        EQ "A"
        AND PR-ALT-IL-R.PR-ALT-ID     EQ DW-COMMON-S.BIL-PR-NPI
        AND PR-ALT-ID-R.PR-ALT-ID-TYP EQ "NP"
        AND PR-ALT-ID-R.BEG-DAT       LE DW-COMMON-S.SRV-BEG-DAT
        IF  PR-ALT-ID-R.END-DAT       GE DW-COMMON-S.SRV-BEG-DAT
          SET BILLING-PROV-NUM = PR-ALT-ID-R.PR-ID
          QUIT BIL-PR-ID 
        ENDIF
      WHEN NONE
       :CONTINUE PROCESSING
    ENDFOR
  
  ENDIF

Note: 
(1) The Billing Provider ID is not an AHCCCS required field.  
(2) If the Billing Provider NPI is present, it will be used to look-up the Billing Provider ID.

column6: The taxonomy code for the institution billing for the beneficiary.
column21:
1. Value must be in Provider Taxonomy List (VVL)
2. Value must be 12 characters or less
3. Conditional
column22:
CAP: "8" FILL (not applicable)

ENC+CLM:
  "8" FILL (default)
  IF BILLING-PROV-NUM NE "8" FILL
    IF DW-COMADD-S.BIL-PR-TAXON-CD GT " "
      FOR FIRST PR-TAXONOMY-R  :<-VALIDATE
        WHERE PR-TAXONOMY-R.STA         EQ "A"
          AND PR-TAXONOMY-R.PR-ID       EQ BILLING-PROV-NUM (1-6)
          AND PR-TAXONOMY-R.TAXONOMY-CD EQ DW-COMADD-S.BIL-PR-TAXON-CD
          AND PR-TAXONOMY-R.END-DAT     GE DW-COMMON-S.SRV-BEG-DAT
            SET BILLING-PROV-TAXONOMY = PR-TAXONOMY-R.TAXONOMY-CD
        WHEN NONE
         :PERFORM ALTERNATE READ  
      ENDFOR
    ELSE
     :PERFORM ALTERNATE READ
    ENDIF
  ENDIF
  Alternate Read:
    IF  BILLING-PROV-NUM NE "8" FILL AND BILLING-PROV-NPI-NUM NE "8" FILL
      FOR EACH PR-TAXONOMY-R
        WHERE PR-TAXONOMY-R.STA         EQ "A"
          AND PR-TAXONOMY-R.PR-ID       EQ BILLING-PROV-NUM (1-6)
          AND PR-TAXONOMY-R.TAXONOMY-CD GT " "
          AND PR-TAXONOMY-R.END-DAT     GE DW-COMMON-S.SRV-BEG-DAT
          IF  PR-TAXONOMY-R.NPI         EQ BILLING-PROV-NPI-NUM (1-10)
          AND PR-TAXONOMY-R.TAX-SW      EQ "Y"
            SET BILLING-PROV-TAXONOMY = PR-TAXONOMY-R.TAXONOMY-CD
            QUIT PR-TAXONOMY-R
          ENDIF
        WHEN NONE
         :CONTINUE PROCESSING
      ENDFOR
    ENDIF

column6: A code to describe the type of provider being reported.
column21:
1. Value must be in Provider Type Code List (VVL).
2. Value must be 2 characters
3. Conditional
column22:
CAP: "8" FILL (not applicable)

ENC+CLM: 

  SET W.BIL-PR-TYP = "8" FILL

  IF BILLING-PROV-NUM NE “8” FILL
     
    FOR FIRST PR-DEM-ID-RC
      WHERE PR-DEM-ID-RC.PR-ID EQ BILLING-PROV-NUM (1-6)

        <<BIL-PR-TYP-TBL>>
        LOOP VARYING N FROM 1 BY 1 THRU W.PR-TYP-TBL-END
          IF PR-DEM-ID-RC.PR-TYP EQ W.A-PR-TYP(N)
            SET BILLING-PROV-TYPE = W.T-PR-TYP(N)
            QUIT BIL-PR-TYP-TBL
          ENDIF
        ENDLOOP   
   
      WHEN NONE
       :CONTINUE PROCESSING
    ENDFOR    
    
   ENDIF

Notes:
(1) The Billing Provider Type is not an AHCCCS required field.
(2) The PR-TYP mapping data can be found in HPMMIS online at RF791 and RF975.

column6:
The Medicare HIC Number (HICN) is an identifier formerly used by SSA and CMS to identify all Medicare beneficiaries. For many beneficiaries, their SSN was a major component of their HICN. To prevent identify theft, among other reasons, HICN gradually were retired and replaced by the 
Medicare Beneficiary Identifier (MBI) over the course of 2018 and 2019. HICN continue to be used by Medicare for limited administrative purposes after 2019 but starting in 2020 the MBI became the primary identifier for Medicare beneficiaries. HICN consists of two components: SSN & 
alpha-suffix or (for Railroad IDs) prefix and ID (not always SSN based)

column6:
The Medicare Beneficiary Identifier (MBI) is a randomly generated identifier used to identify all Medicare beneficiaries. It replaced the previously-used SSN-based Medicare HIC Number (HICN). To prevent identify theft, among other reasons, HICN gradually were retired and replaced by the MBI 
over the course of 2018 and 2019. Starting in 2020, the MBI became the primary identifier for Medicare beneficiaries.
column21:
1. Conditional
2. Value must be an 11-character string
3. Character 1 must be numeric values 1 thru 9
4. Character 2 must be alphabetic values A thru Z (minus S, L, O, I, B, Z)
5. Character 3 must be alphanumeric values 0 thru 9 or A thru Z (minus S, L, O, I, B, Z)
6. Character 4 must be numeric values 0 thru 9
7. Character 5 must be alphabetic values A thru Z (minus S, L, O, I, B, Z)
8. Character 6 must be alphanumeric values 0 thru 9 or A thru Z (minus S, L, O, I, B, Z)
9. Character 7 must be numeric values 0 thru 9
10. Character 8 must be alphabetic values A thru Z (minus S, L, O, I, B, Z)
11. Character 9 must be alphabetic values A thru Z (minus S, L, O, I, B, Z)
12. Character 10 must be numeric values 0 thru 9
13. Character 11 must be numeric values 0 thru 9

column6: The National Provider ID (NPI) of the doctor responsible for admitting a patient to a hospital or other inpatient health facility.
column21:
1. Value must be 10 digits, consisting of 9 numeric digits followed by one check digit calculated using the Luhn formula (algorithm)
2. Value must have an associated Provider Identifier Type equal to '2'
3. Conditional
column22:
"8" FILL (default)

IF DW-COMMON-S.ATND-PR-NPI GT " "
  SET ADMITTING-PROV-NPI-NUM = DW-COMMON-S.ATND-PR-NPI
ELSE 
  IF DW-COMMON-S.ATND-PR-ID GT " "
    <<ATND-PR-NPI>>
    FOR EACH PR-ALT-ID-R
      WHERE PR-ALT-ID-R.PR-STA        EQ "A"
        AND PR-ALT-ID-R.PR-ID         EQ DW-COMMON-S.ATND-PR-ID
        AND PR-ALT-ID-R.BEG-DAT       LE DW-COMMON-S.SRV-BEG-DAT
        IF  PR-ALT-ID-R.END-DAT       GE DW-COMMON-S.SRV-BEG-DAT
        AND PR-ALT-ID-R.PR-ALT-ID-TYP EQ "NP"
          SET ADMITTING-PROV-NPI-NUM = PR-ALT-ID-R.PR-ALT-ID
          QUIT ATND-PR-NPI 
        ENDIF
      WHEN NONE
      :CONTINUE PROCESSING
    ENDFOR
  ENDIF
ENDIF

Notes:
(1) The Admitting Provider and Attending Provider are synonymous as per CMS.
(2) The Admitting Provider NPI is not an AHCCCS required field.

column6: This code describes the area of specialty for the provider being reported.
column21:
1. Value must be in Provider Specialty List (VVL).
2. Value must be 2 characters
3. Conditional
column22:
"8" FILL (default)

IF ADMITTING-PROV-NUM NE "8" FILL
  <<PR-SPEC-ID>>
  FOR EACH PR-SPEC-ID-RC  :<-KEY-1 WILL READ IN PR-ID, SPEC AND BEG-DAT ORDER 
    WHERE PR-SPEC-ID-RC.PR-ID EQ ADMITTING-PROV-NUM (positions 1-6)
      IF PR-SPEC-ID-RC.END-DAT EQ "99999999"      
        <<PR-SPEC-TBL>>
        LOOP VARYING N FROM 1 BY 1 THRU W.PR-SPEC-TBL-END
          IF PR-SPEC-ID-RC.SPEC EQ W.A-PR-SPEC(N)
            SET ADMITTING-PROV-SPECIALTY = W.T-PR-SPEC(N)
            QUIT PR-SPEC-ID
          ENDIF
        ENDLOOP
      ENDIF
    WHEN NONE
     :CONTINUE PROCESSING 
  ENDFOR
ENDIF

Notes:
(1) Not all providers have Specialties.
(2) The PR-SPEC-TBL is the common routine for data provider specialties. 
(3) The W.A-PR-SPEC represents the AHCCCS-defined provider specialties.
(4) The W.T-PR-SPEC represents the TMSIS-defined provider specialties.
(5) The PR-SPEC mapping data can be found in HPMMIS online at RF792 and RF976.

    WHERE PR-TAXONOMY-R.STA         EQ "A"

          QUIT ATND-PR-TAXONOMY
        ENDIF
    WHEN NONE

  ENDFOR
ENDIF

(2) The Attending Provider Taxonomy Code is stored in the Data Warehouse, but not on DWCOMMON or HPMMIS.

column6:
The Record ID represents the type of segment being reported.  The Record ID communicates how the contents of a 
given row of data should be interpreted depending on which segment type the Record ID represents.  Each type of 
segment collects different data elements so each segment type has a distinct layout. The first 3 characters identify 
the relevant file (e.g., ELG, PRV, CIP, etc.). The last 5 digits are the segment identifier padded with leading zeros (e.g., 
00001, 00002, 00003, etc.).
column21:

column6:
A state-assigned unique identification number used to identify a Medicaid/CHIP enrolled individual (except on service tracking payments).  Value may be an SSN, temporary SSN or State-assigned eligible individual identifier.

MSIS Identification Numbers are a unique "key" value used to maintain referential integrity of data distributed over multiples files, segments and reporting periods.

See T-MSIS Guidance Document, "CMS Guidance:  Reporting Shared MSIS Identification Numbers" for information on reporting the MSIS Identification Numbers ID for pregnant women, unborn children, mothers, and their deemed newborns younger than 1 year of age who share the 
same MSIS Identification Number.
https://www.medicaid.gov/medicaid/data-and-systems/macbis/tmsis/tmsis-blog/entry/47572
column21:
1. Mandatory
2. For SSN States (i.e. SSN Indicator = 1), value must be equal to eligible individual's SSN
3. For Non-SSN States (i.e. SSN Indicator = 0), value must not be equal to eligible individual's SSN
4. Value must be 20 characters or less
5. When Type of Claim (CLT.002.052) equals 4, D or X (lump sum payment) value must begin with an '&'

column6:
For services received during a single encounter with a provider, the date the service covered by this claim was received. For services involving multiple encounters on different days, or periods of care extending over two or more days, this would be the date on which the service 
covered by this claim began. For capitation premium payments, the date on which the period of coverage related to this payment began. For financial transactions reported to the OT file, populate with the first day of the time period covered by this financial transaction.
column21:
1. Value must be 8 characters in the form "CCYYMMDD"
2. The date must be a valid calendar date (i.e. Feb 29th only on the leap year,  never April 31st or Sept 31st)
3. When Type of Claim is not in ['2', '4', 'B', 'D', 'V'] value must be less than or equal to associated End of Time Period value
4. Value must be less than or equal to associated Ending Date of Service value
5. When Type of Claim is not in ['2', '4', 'B', 'D', 'V'] value must be less than or equal to associated Adjudication Date value
6. Value must be less than or equal to associated Date of Death (ELG.002.025) value when populated
7. Value must be less than or equal to at least one of the eligible's Enrollment End Date (ELG.021.254) values
8. Mandatory
column26: TMSIS - 2019 Hawaii Source to Target Mapping Matrix.xlsx (sharepoint.com) , Data Guide from TMSIS Website Data Guide (medicaid.gov)

column6:
For services received during a single encounter with a provider, the date the service covered by this claim was received. For services involving multiple encounters on different days, or periods of care extending over two or more days, the date on which the service covered by this claim ended. For capitation premium payments, the date on 
which the period of coverage related to this payment ends/ended.  For financial transactions reported to the OT file, populate with the last day of the time period covered by this financial transaction.
column21:
1. Value must be 8 characters in the form "CCYYMMDD"
2. The date must be a valid calendar date (i.e. Feb 29th only on the leap year,  never April 31st or Sept 31st)
3. When Type of Claim is not in ['2', '4', 'B', 'D', 'V'] value must be less than or equal to associated End of Time Period value
4. Value must be greater than or equal to associated Beginning Date of Service value
5. When Type of Claim is not in ['2', '4', 'B', 'D', 'V'] value must be less than or equal to associated Adjudication Date value
6. Value must be less than or equal to associated Date of Death (ELG.002.025) value when populated
7. Value must be equal to or greater than associated Date of Birth (ELG.002.024) value
8. Mandatory

This field is only applicable when the service being billed can be quantified in discrete units, e.g., a number of visits or the number of units of a prescription/refill that were filled. column6: A code to categorize the services provided to a Medicaid or CHIP enrollee.
column22:
When the program is first initialized, read the following data into a working storage table:

  SET N = 0

  FOR EACH RFGT2RC
    WHERE RFGT2RC.STA-CD  EQ "A"
      AND RFGT2RC.TBL-TYP EQ "RF970"
        ADD 1 TO N
        SET W.COS(N) = RFGT2RC.TBL-CD   <- 2-byte AHCCCS Category of Service
        SET W.TOS(N) = RFGT2RC.TBL-DESC <- 3-byte TMSIS Type of Service
  ENDFOR 

  SET W.COS-TOS-TBL-END = N

Find the TMSISIP.TYPE-OF-SERVICE using the DW-COMMON-S.CLM-COS:

  SET TMSISIP.TYP-OF-SERVICE = "999" (default)

  <<COS-TOS-TBL>>
  LOOP VARYING N FROM 1 BY 1 THRU W.COS-TOS-TBL-END
    IF DW-COMMON-S.CLM-COS EQ W.COS(N)
      SET TMSISIP.TYPE-OF-SERVICE = W.TOS(N)
      QUIT COS-TOS-TBL
    ENDIF
  ENDLOOP   

Note: The COS-TOS mapping data can be found in HPMMIS online at RF970.
column26: TMSIS - 2019 Hawaii Source to Target Mapping Matrix.xlsx (sharepoint.com) , Data Guide from TMSIS Website Data Guide (medicaid.gov)

The NPI of the health care professional who delivers or completes a particular medical service or non-surgical procedure.  The Servicing Provider NPI Number is required when rendering provider is different than the attending provider and state or federal regulatory requirements call for 
a "combined claim" (i.e., a claim that includes both facility and professional components).  Examples are Medicaid clinic bills or critical access hospital claims.
column21:
1. Value must be 10 digits, consisting of 9 numeric digits followed by one check digit calculated using the Luhn formula (algorithm)
2. Value must have an associated Provider Identifier Type equal to '2'
3. Conditional
4. When Type of Claim (CLT.002.052) not in ('3','C','W') then value must match Provider Identifier (PRV.005.081)

column22:
"8" FILL (default)

IF DW-COMMON-S.SER-PR-ID GT " "
  IF DW-COMADD-S.SER-PR-TAXON-CD GT " "
    FOR FIRST PR-TAXONOMY-R  :<-VALIDATE
      WHERE PR-TAXONOMY-R.STA         EQ "A"
        AND PR-TAXONOMY-R.PR-ID       EQ DW-COMMON-S.SER-PR-ID
        AND PR-TAXONOMY-R.TAXONOMY-CD EQ DW-COMADD-S.SER-PR-TAXON-CD
        AND PR-TAXONOMY-R.END-DAT     GE DW-COMMON-S.SRV-BEG-DAT
          SET SERVICING-PROV-TAXONOMY = PR-TAXONOMY-R.TAXONOMY-CD
      WHEN NONE
       :PERFORM ALTERNATE READ  
    ENDFOR
  ELSE
   :PERFORM ALTERNATE READ
  ENDIF
ENDIF

ALTERNATE-READ:
  IF DW-COMMON-S.SER-PR-ID GT " " AND DW-COMMON-S.SER-PR-NPI GT " "
    FOR EACH PR-TAXONOMY-R
      WHERE PR-TAXONOMY-R.STA         EQ "A"
        AND PR-TAXONOMY-R.PR-ID       EQ DW-COMMON-S.SER-PR-ID
        AND PR-TAXONOMY-R.TAXONOMY-CD GT " "
        AND PR-TAXONOMY-R.END-DAT     GE DW-COMMON-S.SRV-BEG-DAT
          IF  PR-TAXONOMY-R.NPI       EQ DW-COMMON-S.SER-PR-NPI
          AND PR-TAXONOMY-R.TAX-SW    EQ "Y"
            SET SERVICING-PROV-TAXONOMY = PR-TAXONOMY-R.TAXONOMY-CD
            QUIT PR-TAXONOMY-R
          ENDIF
      WHEN NONE
       :CONTINUE PROCESSING
    ENDFOR
  ENDIF

Note: The Taxonomy is not an AHCCCS required field.

        AND PR-TAXONOMY-R.TAXONOMY-CD GT " "
        AND PR-TAXONOMY-R.END-DAT     GE DW-COMMON-S.SRV-BEG-DAT

            QUIT PR-TAXONOMY-R

       :CONTINUE PROCESSING
    ENDFOR
  ENDIF

given row of data should be interpreted depending on which segment type the Record ID represents.  Each type of

column22:
"COT00002"

OT File Processing Changes:

Currently, COT00002 records represent header-level information and COT00003 records represent detail-level information.  This is not changing but the association between the COT000002 and COT00003 records is going to change for Professional and Dental encounters.

In the Claim system when an adjustment is submitted, all of the lines associated with the CLM-NO are re-processed and re-adjudicated.  This is true for all form types.  As a result, when a claim is identified as an adjustment during the OT process, the adjustment designation applies not only to the header-level COT00002 record, but to all of the associated line-level COT00003 records.  This is not the case for Professional and Dental encounters.

In the Encounter system when an adjustment is submitted for a Professional or Dental encounter, the adjustment is assigned at the line-level (CRN).  As a result, when an encounter is identified as an adjustment during the OT process, the adjustment designation applies only to the detail-level COT0003 record.  This can result in an issue as it relates to the COT00002 record.  If there are multiple related COT00003 records present and they are not all adjustments, what is the appropriate designation for the COT00002 record?

To resolve this potential issue, we will treat each individual Professional and Dental encounter line as a unique encounter and populate both the COT0002 and COT0003 ICN fields with a CRN.  If the detail line is identified as an adjustment, it will be noted accordingly on both records.

As a result, when a claim is identified as an adjustment during the OT process, the adjustment designation applies not only to the header-level COT00002 record, but to all of the associated line-level COT00003 records.  This is not the case for Professional and Dental encounters.

This can result in an issue as it relates to the COT00002 record. MSIS Identification Numbers are a unique "key" value used to maintain referential integrity of data distributed over multiples files, segments and reporting periods.

      FOR FIRST EC-MED-CAS-R
        WHERE EC-MED-CAS-R.CRN            EQ DW-COMMON-S.CRN
          AND EC-MED-CAS-R.OTH-PYR-PRI-ID EQ W.OTH-PYR-PRI-ID
          AND EC-MED-CAS-R.SEQ-NO         EQ "001"
            SET ADJUSTMENT-REASON-CODE = EC-MED-CAS-R.ADJ-RSN-CD
        WHEN NONE
         :CONTINUE PROCESSING
      ENDFOR  

    Dental:
      FOR FIRST EC-DNT-CAS-R
        WHERE EC-DNT-CAS-R.CRN            EQ DW-COMMON-S.CRN
          AND EC-DNT-CAS-R.OTH-PYR-PRI-ID EQ W.OTH-PYR-PRI-ID
          AND EC-DNT-CAS-R.SEQ-NO         EQ "001"
            SET ADJUSTMENT-REASON-CODE = EC-DNT-CAS-R.ADJ-RSN-CD
        WHEN NONE
         :CONTINUE PROCESSING
      ENDFOR  

    Outpatient: "8" FILL (not applicable at the header level - See LINE-ADJUSTMENT-REASON-CODE (COT163)

  ENDIF

column6:
A code to identify conditions that are present at the time the order for inpatient admission occurs; conditions that develop during an outpatient encounter, including emergency department, observation, or outpatient surgery.
POA indicator is used to identify certain preventable conditions that are: (a) high cost or high volume or both, (b) result in the assignment of a case to a Diagnosis Related Group (DRG)* that has a higher payment when present as a secondary diagnosis, and (c) could reasonably 
have been prevented through the application of evidence-based guidelines.
*States that do not use the grouper methodology may use CMS-approved methodology that is prospective in nature.
Each Diagnosis Code Flag is associated with one, and only one, Diagnosis Code in a given file segment record.  For example, Diagnosis Code n is associated with Diagnosis Code Flag n, where n can be any integer greater than or equal to 1.

  Outpatient: 
    Valid Values:
      "Y" = Diagnosis was present at time of inpatient admission
      "N" = Diagnosis was not present at time of inpatient admission
      "U" = Documentation insufficient to determine if condition was present at time of inpatient admission
      "W" = Clinically undetermined
    BLANK = Exempt from POA reporting (default)

Notes:
(1) This field is only applicable to Outpatient records and only under specific conditions.  Since BLANK is a valid value, the default value for Outpatient records is a SPACE. The field should be populated with whatever value is present; no editing required.
(2) Since a value of "1" was a valid value at one time and has the same meaning as a BLANK entry, any POA field reported to AHCCCS with a value of "1" will be converted to a BLANK for TMSIS reporting.
